title: Connect to BigQuery
text: get access OpenPrescribing BigQuery database write public Slack channel tag @bigquery_admins. One admins send JSON file BigQuery credentials private message. file look something like : Never commit file GitHub share anyone.
code: {   "type": "ACCOUNT_TYPE",   "project_id": "PROJECT_ID",   "private_key_id": "PRIVATE_KEY_ID",   "private_key": "PRIVATE_KEY",   "client_email": "CLIENT_EMAIL",   "client_id": "CLIENT_ID",   "auth_uri": "AUTH_URI",   "token_uri": "TOKEN_URI",   "auth_provider_x509_cert_url": "AUTH_CERTIFICATE",   "client_x509_cert_url": "CLIENT_CERTIFICATE",   "universe_domain": "DOMAIN" }

title: Connect to BigQuery
text: can store file anywhere computer, common location hidden folder home directory, example: macOS/Linux: Windows:
code: ~/.config/openprescribing/bq-credentials.json C:/Users/YOUR_USERNAME/.config/openprescribing/bq-credentials.json

title: Connect to BigQuery
text: Rather hardcoding path R scripts can set environment variable named OP_CREDENTIALS. help edit .Renviron file set variable R sessions can use usethis package: open .Renviron file editor can add location stored credentials file, example: Note: probably need restart R session R know new environment variable.
code: # Open your .Renviron file usethis::edit_r_environ() # macOS/Linux OP_CREDENTIALS=~/.config/openprescribing/bq-credentials.json  # Windows OP_CREDENTIALS=C:/Users/YOUR_USERNAME/.config/openprescribing/bq-credentials.json

title: Connect to BigQuery
text: environment variable place, connect_bq() authenticates connects hscic dataset ebmdatalab project. might asked log Google account first time run . can connect different dataset, use differently named environment variable, via arguments: connection works everything DBI dplyr/dbplyr ecosystem, example: something set correctly, connect_bq() tell fix, example environment variable missing:
code: library(opr)  con <- connect_bq() con <- connect_bq(dataset = "dmd", credentials_var = "MY_OTHER_CREDENTIALS") # List all available tables DBI::dbListTables(con)  # Create a lazy query against any table dplyr::tbl(con, "practices") opr::connect_bq(credentials_var = "VARIABLE_THAT_IS_NOT_SET") #> Error in `opr::connect_bq()`: #> ! Environment variable `VARIABLE_THAT_IS_NOT_SET` is not set. #> ℹ Set it in your .Renviron file to point to your BigQuery credentials JSON #>   file. #> ℹ You can edit your .Renviron file using `usethis::edit_r_environ()`. #> ℹ See `vignette("connect-bigquery", package = "opr")` for detailed setup #>   instructions.

title: Connect to BigQuery
text: See vignette("opr") query data get_*() functions, vignette("database-schema") descriptions main tables.

title: Database schema
text: cleaned-version main prescribing dataset, updated monthly. table usually want, rather raw prescribing table. Query get_normalised_prescribing().
code: normalised_prescribing_sample #> # A tibble: 5 × 12 #>   sha   regional_team stp   pct   practice bnf_code      bnf_name items net_cost #>   <chr> <chr>         <chr> <chr> <chr>    <chr>         <chr>    <int>    <dbl> #> 1 Q44   Y62           QYG   12F   N85625   0302000ABBBA… Fostair…    30    880.  #> 2 Q44   Y62           QYG   02E   N81089   0401020K0AAA… Diazepa…    63     48.8 #> 3 Q44   Y62           QYG   12F   N85012   0302000K0BHA… DuoResp…    33   1079.  #> 4 Q44   Y62           QYG   27D   Y03882   23804708006   Proshie…    55    449.  #> 5 Q44   Y62           QYG   12F   N85054   0205052N0AAA… Losarta…    22     33.3 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: actual_cost <dbl>, quantity <dbl>, month <dttm>

title: Database schema
text: GP practice details including addresses organisational information, updated monthly. Contains latest month’s data , includes closed practices. Query get_practices().
code: practices_sample #> # A tibble: 5 × 17 #>   code   name     address1 address2 address3 address4 address5 postcode location #>   <chr>  <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    #> 1 A81001 THE DEN… HEALTH … LAWSON … NA       STOCKTO… NA       TS18 1HU 0101000… #> 2 A81002 QUEENS … FARRER … NA       NA       STOCKTO… NA       TS18 2AW 0101000… #> 3 A81003 VICTORI… HEALTH … VICTORI… NA       HARTLEP… NA       TS26 8DB 0101000… #> 4 A81004 ACKLAM … TRIMDON… ACKLAM   NA       MIDDLES… NA       TS5 8SB  0101000… #> 5 A81005 SPRINGW… RECTORY… NA       NA       GUISBOR… NA       TS14 7DJ 0101000… #> # ℹ 8 more variables: ccg_id <chr>, pcn_id <chr>, setting <int>, #> #   close_date <chr>, join_provider_date <chr>, leave_provider_date <chr>, #> #   open_date <chr>, status_code <chr>

title: Database schema
text: Practice-level patient counts age gender, updated monthly. Contains latest month’s data . Also contains population weightings used compare prescribing across practices different patient populations (STAR-PU ASTRO-PU).
code: practice_statistics_sample #> # A tibble: 5 × 25 #>   month               male_0_4 female_0_4 male_5_14 male_15_24 male_25_34 #>   <dttm>                 <int>      <int>     <int>      <int>      <int> #> 1 2017-07-01 00:00:00       12         12        27         45         81 #> 2 2017-07-01 00:00:00      106         67       172        177        374 #> 3 2017-07-01 00:00:00      145        159       442        436        478 #> 4 2017-07-01 00:00:00      224        206       510        395        723 #> 5 2017-07-01 00:00:00      288        222       700        465        738 #> # ℹ 19 more variables: male_35_44 <int>, male_45_54 <int>, male_55_64 <int>, #> #   male_65_74 <int>, male_75_plus <int>, female_5_14 <int>, #> #   female_15_24 <int>, female_25_34 <int>, female_35_44 <int>, #> #   female_45_54 <int>, female_55_64 <int>, female_65_74 <int>, #> #   female_75_plus <int>, total_list_size <int>, astro_pu_cost <dbl>, #> #   astro_pu_items <dbl>, star_pu <chr>, pct_id <chr>, practice <chr>

title: Database schema
text: Names open/close dates regional teams.
code: regional_teams_sample #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   code  name                                              open_date  close_date #>   <chr> <chr>                                             <date>     <date>     #> 1 Y54   NORTH OF ENGLAND COMMISSIONING REGION             2012-10-01 2019-03-31 #> 2 Y55   MIDLANDS AND EAST OF ENGLAND COMMISSIONING REGION 2012-10-01 2019-03-31 #> 3 Y56   LONDON COMMISSIONING REGION                       2012-10-01 NA         #> 4 Y57   SOUTH OF ENGLAND COMMISSIONING REGION             2012-10-01 2018-03-31 #> 5 Y58   SOUTH WEST COMMISSIONING REGION                   2018-04-01 NA

title: Database schema
text: normalised_prescribing$practice matches practices$code normalised_prescribing$regional_team matches regional_teams$code practice_statistics$practice matches practices$code See “Joining tables” section vignette("opr") worked example.

title: Getting started with opr
code: con <- connect_bq()

title: Getting started with opr
text: get_practices() queries practices table, options add descriptive labels setting status_code columns filter . description table see vignette("database-schema"), look bundled five-row sample: labels come lookup tables shipped package: example, get standard GP practices (setting code 4) labels setting status codes: see SQL run BigQuery, use show_query():
code: practices_sample |>   select(code, name, postcode, setting, status_code) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   code   name                       postcode setting status_code #>   <chr>  <chr>                      <chr>      <int> <chr>       #> 1 A81001 THE DENSHAM SURGERY        TS18 1HU       4 A           #> 2 A81002 QUEENS PARK MEDICAL CENTRE TS18 2AW       4 A           #> 3 A81003 VICTORIA MEDICAL PRACTICE  TS26 8DB       4 B           #> 4 A81004 ACKLAM MEDICAL CENTRE      TS5 8SB        4 A           #> 5 A81005 SPRINGWOOD SURGERY         TS14 7DJ       4 A practice_settings #> # A tibble: 24 × 2 #>    setting setting_label            #>      <int> <chr>                    #>  1       0 Other                    #>  2       1 WIC Practice             #>  3       2 OOH Practice             #>  4       3 WIC + OOH Practice       #>  5       4 GP Practice              #>  6       8 Public Health Service    #>  7       9 Community Health Service #>  8      10 Hospital Service         #>  9      11 Optometry Service        #> 10      12 Urgent & Emergency Care  #> # ℹ 14 more rows  practice_statuses #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   status_code status_code_label #>   <chr>       <chr>             #> 1 A           Active            #> 2 B           Retired           #> 3 C           Closed            #> 4 D           Dormant           #> 5 P           Proposed          #> 6 U           Unknown get_practices(   con,   add_setting_labels = TRUE,   filter_setting = 4,   add_status_code_labels = TRUE ) |>   select(code, name, setting_label, status_code_label) |>   collect() get_practices(con, add_setting_labels = TRUE, filter_setting = 4) |>   select(code, name, setting_label) |>   show_query()

title: Getting started with opr
text: get_normalised_prescribing() queries normalised_prescribing table optional filtering BNF codes prescribing month. full table large, filter collecting. sample table: example get prescribing data two BNF codes January March 2023:
code: normalised_prescribing_sample |>   select(month, practice, bnf_code, bnf_name, items, quantity) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>   month               practice bnf_code        bnf_name           items quantity #>   <dttm>              <chr>    <chr>           <chr>              <int>    <dbl> #> 1 2016-12-01 00:00:00 N85625   0302000ABBBAAAA Fostair NEXThaler…    30       30 #> 2 2016-12-01 00:00:00 N81089   0401020K0AAAHAH Diazepam 2mg tabl…    63     1822 #> 3 2016-12-01 00:00:00 N85012   0302000K0BHAAAM DuoResp Spiromax …    33       36 #> 4 2016-12-01 00:00:00 Y03882   23804708006     Proshield Foam & …    55    15980 #> 5 2016-12-01 00:00:00 N85054   0205052N0AAADAD Losartan 100mg ta…    22      896 get_normalised_prescribing(   con,   bnf_codes = c("0408010W0BBAKA1", "0601022B0AAASAS"),   start_date = "2023-01-01",   end_date = "2023-03-01" ) |>   collect()

title: Getting started with opr
text: Joins also run BigQuery anything downloaded. join prescribing data GP practices filter metformin:
code: # Metformin 500mg modified-release tablets in March 2025 prescribing_query <- get_normalised_prescribing(   con,   bnf_codes = "0601022B0AAASAS",   start_date = "2025-03-01",   end_date = "2025-03-01" ) |>   select(month, practice, bnf_code, bnf_name, items, quantity)  practices_query <- get_practices(con, filter_setting = 4) |>   select(code, name, status_code)  prescribing_query |>   inner_join(practices_query, by = c("practice" = "code")) |>   collect()

title: Getting started with opr
text: get_*() functions cover commonly used tables. connection connect_bq() works table dataset: prefer writing SQL directly, works :
code: dplyr::tbl(con, "practice_statistics") DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT code, name FROM practices LIMIT 5")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Milan Wiedemann. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wiedemann M (2026). opr: OpenPrescribing R. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/bennettoxford/opr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {opr: OpenPrescribing in R},   author = {Milan Wiedemann},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/bennettoxford/opr}, }

title: OpenPrescribing in R
text: opr package helps researchers access analyse OpenPrescribing.net BigQuery database R. Instead writing SQL, can use dplyr.

title: OpenPrescribing in R
text: can install development version opr GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("bennettoxford/opr")

title: OpenPrescribing in R
text: ’ll need BigQuery credentials, see Connect BigQuery setup instructions. get_*() functions return query runs BigQuery; nothing downloaded call collect(). See Getting started details.
code: library(opr) library(dplyr)  con <- connect_bq()  # Standard GP practices (setting code 4) with descriptive labels get_practices(con, filter_setting = 4, add_setting_labels = TRUE) |>   collect()  # Prescribing of metformin 500mg MR tablets in early 2023 get_normalised_prescribing(   con,   bnf_codes = "0601022B0AAASAS",   start_date = "2023-01-01",   end_date = "2023-03-01" ) |>   collect()

title: OpenPrescribing in R
text: See DEVELOPERS.md.

what: Connect to the OpenPrescribing BigQuery database — connect_bq
title: Connect to the OpenPrescribing BigQuery database — connect_bq
text: Authenticates BigQuery using service account credentials file returns database connection. path credentials file read environment variable (default OP_CREDENTIALS), credentials never need hardcoded analysis scripts.

title: Connect to the OpenPrescribing BigQuery database — connect_bq
code: connect_bq(   project = "ebmdatalab",   dataset = "hscic",   billing = "ebmdatalab",   credentials_var = "OP_CREDENTIALS",   quiet = FALSE )

title: Connect to the OpenPrescribing BigQuery database — connect_bq
text: project Character, BigQuery project. Default "ebmdatalab". dataset Character, BigQuery dataset connect . Default "hscic". billing Character, BigQuery project bill. Default "ebmdatalab". credentials_var Character, name environment variable holds path BigQuery credentials JSON file. Default "OP_CREDENTIALS". quiet Logical, suppress informational messages. Default FALSE.

title: Connect to the OpenPrescribing BigQuery database — connect_bq
text: DBIConnection BigQuery can used DBI::dbGetQuery(), dplyr::tbl(), get_*() functions package.

title: Connect to the OpenPrescribing BigQuery database — connect_bq
text: See vignette("connect-bigquery", package = "opr") get credentials set environment variable.

title: Connect to the OpenPrescribing BigQuery database — connect_bq
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- connect_bq()  # List all available tables DBI::dbListTables(con)  # Disconnect when done DBI::dbDisconnect(con) } # }

title: Get normalised prescribing — get_normalised_prescribing
text: Queries normalised_prescribing table, optional filtering BNF codes prescribing month. table usually want, rather raw prescribing table.

title: Get normalised prescribing — get_normalised_prescribing
code: get_normalised_prescribing(   con,   bnf_codes = NULL,   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL )

title: Get normalised prescribing — get_normalised_prescribing
text: con BigQuery connection connect_bq(). bnf_codes Character vector BNF codes keep, e.g. "0601022B0AAASAS". Default NULL (filter). start_date Earliest prescribing month keep, Date "YYYY-MM-DD" string. Default NULL (filter). end_date Latest prescribing month keep, Date "YYYY-MM-DD" string. Default NULL (filter).

title: Get normalised prescribing — get_normalised_prescribing
text: query (tbl) normalised_prescribing table, downloaded dplyr::collect().

title: Get normalised prescribing — get_normalised_prescribing
text: result query, data: nothing downloaded call dplyr::collect(), can add dplyr steps first. full table large, filter BNF code date range collecting.

title: Get normalised prescribing — get_normalised_prescribing
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- connect_bq()  # Prescribing of two presentations between January and March 2023 get_normalised_prescribing(   con,   bnf_codes = c("0408010W0BBAKA1", "0601022B0AAASAS"),   start_date = "2023-01-01",   end_date = "2023-03-01" ) |>   dplyr::collect() } # }

title: Get practices — get_practices
text: Queries practices table, options add descriptive labels setting status_code columns filter columns. Labels defined practice_settings practice_statuses lookup tables.

title: Get practices — get_practices
code: get_practices(   con,   add_setting_labels = FALSE,   filter_setting = NULL,   add_status_code_labels = FALSE,   filter_status_code = NULL )

title: Get practices — get_practices
text: con BigQuery connection connect_bq(). add_setting_labels Logical, add setting_label column describing practice setting code. Default FALSE. filter_setting Integer vector setting codes keep, e.g. 4 standard GP practices. Default NULL (filter). See practice_settings. add_status_code_labels Logical, add status_code_label column describing practice status code. Default FALSE. filter_status_code Character vector status codes keep, e.g. "" active practices. Default NULL (filter). See practice_statuses.

title: Get practices — get_practices
text: query (tbl) practices table, downloaded dplyr::collect().

title: Get practices — get_practices
text: result query, data: nothing downloaded call dplyr::collect(), can add dplyr steps first.

title: Get practices — get_practices
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- connect_bq()  # All practices, unfiltered get_practices(con)  # Standard GP practices with labels, downloaded into R gp_practices <- get_practices(   con,   add_setting_labels = TRUE,   filter_setting = 4 ) |>   dplyr::collect() } # }

title: opr: OpenPrescribing in R — opr-package
text: Provides functions connect query OpenPrescribing.net BigQuery database. Helps researchers understand available data write readable, reusable, modular queries analysing English prescribing data using 'dplyr' 'dbplyr' instead SQL.

title: opr: OpenPrescribing in R — opr-package
text: Maintainer: Milan Wiedemann milan.wiedemann@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Milan Wiedemann milan.wiedemann@gmail.com (ORCID)
